comment: Quality Gates catalog -- orphan-scripts waivers. SIMPLIFY-12 (P63) audits scripts/ at milestone close. Each row documents a script that survives audit (after caller-scan) as a thin shim because canonical removal would break documented dev-loop UX or external doc references, OR as KEEP-AS-CANONICAL because the script has no canonical home under quality/gates/<dim>/. Pure deletions land with no row here. Schema: id=orphan-scripts/<basename>, kind=mechanical, verifier=quality/gates/structure/orphan-scripts-audit.py. D-CONV-3 (2026-07-04): converted scalar `cadence` -> `cadences` list (this catalog now participates in quality/runners/run.py + verdict.py like every other catalog -- see quality/PROTOCOL.md and the run.py/verdict.py discover_catalogs() comments for the removed special-case). Wave 2 (P63) retirement decisions: quality/reports/audits/scripts-retirement-p63.md. D-CONV-3 retirement decisions (shims + one-shot phase scripts deleted, duplicate-pair collapsed to the dash validator): quality/SURPRISES.md.

asserts: scripts/banned-words-lint.sh exists
first 10 lines mention canonical / source-of-truth (docs/.banned-words.toml)
.github/workflows/docs.yml continues to invoke this exact path

rows.owner_hint: if RED: scripts/banned-words-lint.sh grew beyond cap OR docs/.banned-words.toml drifted from the script's hardcoded mirror. CI workflow .github/workflows/docs.yml depends on this path; do NOT delete without updating the workflow.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If scripts/banned-words-lint.sh had actually been deleted or grown past the line cap, the verifier's file-exists check or its `len(lines) > max_lines` comparison would fail and flip this row to FAIL; if the header lost its canonical/source-of-truth mention, the header-hint regex scan would find no match and fail the same way -- so the claim cannot silently hold while the file drifts.

rows.owner_hint: if RED: shim grew OR canonical moved. docs/benchmarks/token-economy.md documents this exact entry point.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If scripts/bench_token_economy.py stopped being a thin shim (grew past 40 lines) or its header lost the canonical-path mention, the verifier's line-count assert or header-hint regex would fail and flip the row to FAIL -- the shim-shape claim cannot silently hold while the file balloons or the pointer comment is deleted.

rows.owner_hint: if RED: scripts/catalog.py grew beyond 500 LOC OR the SIMPLIFY-03 boundary needs revisiting. KEEP-AS-CANONICAL: per-FILE planning aid; structurally distinct from quality/catalogs/ per-CHECK enforcement.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If scripts/catalog.py were deleted, grew past 500 lines, or lost its meta-helper/no-canonical-home header comment, the verifier's exists-check, line-count assert, or header-hint regex scan would each independently fail and flip the row to FAIL rather than silently keeping PASS.

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If scripts/check-quality-catalogs.py were deleted or grew past 200 lines, or its header lost the meta-helper/schema-validator wording, the verifier's exists-check, line-count assert, or header-hint regex would fail and flip the row to FAIL -- so the claim that this script is a live, capped, self-documenting meta-helper cannot silently go stale.

asserts: all six .claude/hooks/*.sh files exist
first 10 lines of each name the wiring: no canonical home under quality/gates/ -- wired via .claude/settings.json
kind == mechanical and dimension == meta

rows.owner_hint: if RED: sources[0] (cargo-mutex.sh) was deleted, grew past 34 lines, or lost its header wiring-comment. KEEP-AS-CANONICAL: Claude Code project hooks; wired via .claude/settings.json hooks block, out of scope for the scripts/ inverse-scan (that walk covers scripts/ only). Row added per owner decision Q7 (forward-proofing) so the six-hook set has a registry entry even though .claude/hooks/ is not scanned by orphan-scripts-audit.py --inverse-scan. max_lines=34 is the longest hook (session-start-brief.sh, 26 lines) + ~30% headroom; the verifier itself only checks sources[0].
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If .claude/hooks/cargo-mutex.sh (sources[0], the row the verifier actually inspects) were deleted, grew past 34 lines, or lost its 'no canonical home ... wired via .claude/settings.json' header comment, the verifier's exists-check, line-count assert, or header-hint regex scan would each independently fail and flip this row to FAIL -- the claim cannot silently hold while the file drifts.
